name: Chicken Scarpariella
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 lb Broiler-fryer chicken, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry the chicken pieces.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
4. Brown the chicken pieces until browned on all sides.
5. Add garlic and lower the heat to medium-low. Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Drain off excess fat from the chicken pieces.
7. Stir in parsley, rosemary, and white wine. Bring mixture back to a simmer.
8. Reduce heat to low and let the sauce simmer until the wine has evaporated and the chicken is cooked through, about 15 minutes.
9. Remove the chicken to a platter.
10. In the same skillet, add whipping cream. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until reduced by half and thickened, about 5-7 minutes. Scrape pan drippings into the sauce.
11. Return the chicken to the skillet and cook for an additional 3-4 minutes or until heated through.
12. Sprinkle with grated parmesan cheese.
13. Serve over hot spaghetti or noodles.
14. Garnish with lemon wedges and mint sprigs.
